assistantUi.heading: VLLNT UI vs assistant-ui
assistantUi.intro: assistant-ui gives you an opinionated chat thread fast. VLLNT UI is a broader, agent-first design system: the same chat surfaces, plus the tool calls, agent activity, citations, artifacts, and product UI an AI-first app needs — all installed via the shadcn CLI so you own the source.
assistantUi.metaDescription: VLLNT UI vs assistant-ui: a full AI-first design system you own via the shadcn CLI vs a focused chat-thread library. Honest comparison of scope, install, and theming.

agentRegistry.vllnt: Yes — llms.txt, llms-full.txt, per-component JSON, MCP

beyondChat.vllnt: Tool calls, agent activity, citations, model comparison, artifacts, dashboards

installModel.vllnt: shadcn CLI — source copied into your repo, yours to edit

scope.other: Focused on the chat/assistant thread experience
scope.vllnt: Full design system — chat plus agents, artifacts, and 200+ UI components

theming.vllnt: CSS variables + design tokens shared across the whole product

whenToPick.other: You want an opinionated chat thread fast
whenToPick.vllnt: You're building a whole AI-first product, not just a chat box

index.intro: Honest comparisons. We call out where alternatives are stronger, where VLLNT UI fits better, and where the gap is small enough to not matter.
index.metaDescription: Honest, evidence-based comparison of VLLNT UI vs shadcn/ui, Radix UI, HeadlessUI, and NextUI.

index.title: VLLNT UI vs the rest

shadcn.metaDescription: VLLNT UI vs shadcn/ui — registry format, component count, agent surface, theming, accessibility. Honest comparison.

a11ySchema.vllnt: Yes — keyboard map, ARIA roles, focus model

ownSource.attribute: You own the source after install

templates.vllnt: Curated app templates for Next.js, dashboards, SaaS, AI chat, and docs

shadcn.title: VLLNT UI vs shadcn/ui
shadcn.tldr1: shadcn/ui is the industry standard. VLLNT UI is a younger sibling that takes the same registry idea further: more components, an agent-first surface (/llms.txt, /mcp, richer JSON descriptors), and a hybrid install model that dedupes shared primitives via @vllnt/ui.
shadcn.tldr2: If you want maximum community signal and the largest pool of third-party tutorials and templates, shadcn/ui wins. If you want the broadest curated component set and a registry that AI agents can read directly, VLLNT UI wins.
shadcn.tldr3: VLLNT UI now includes a <link>starter kit gallery</link> for finished app shells built from the registry.
shadcn.when1: Pick shadcn/ui when you want the most-validated choice, need a large pool of tutorials, or are building something that benefits from being “just shadcn”.
shadcn.when2: Pick VLLNT UI when you need components beyond the shadcn core (timelines, maps, AI compounds, runtime overlays, canvas primitives), when AI agents are part of your authoring flow, or when you want the registry data structured enough to query programmatically.
shadcn.when3: The honest answer: many projects use both. shadcn/ui as the baseline, VLLNT UI for the families shadcn doesn’t cover. Same install model: they coexist cleanly.

vercelAiSdk.buildCta: Build an AI chat UI with VLLNT UI
vercelAiSdk.heading: VLLNT UI vs Vercel AI SDK
vercelAiSdk.intro: These aren't competitors — they're layers. The Vercel AI SDK is the data layer that streams tokens and talks to model providers. VLLNT UI is the design system that renders the result: chat input, message bubbles, streaming text, tool calls, citations, and artifacts. Most AI apps want both.
vercelAiSdk.metaDescription: VLLNT UI vs the Vercel AI SDK: a UI design system you own vs a model/streaming runtime. They solve different problems and compose together. Honest comparison.
vercelAiSdk.metaTitle: VLLNT UI vs Vercel AI SDK

bestTogether.other: Use the AI SDK for the data layer — they compose cleanly
bestTogether.vllnt: Use VLLNT UI for the interface

componentBreadth.vllnt: Full design system — chat plus the rest of the product UI

ownSource.vllnt: Yes — installed via shadcn CLI into your repo

primaryJob.other: How the AI app talks to models and streams responses

whatItIs.other: A runtime/data layer — streaming, useChat, model providers
whatItIs.vllnt: A UI design system — presentational React components you own
